steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says genetic variations explain behavioral differences between breeds, and this is especially pronounced for trainability. The correct choice must follow directly from that claim: if trainability differences are genetic, and two breeds differ in trainability, they differ genetically in trainability.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — future genetic changes: shifts to predicting how genetics will change, which the passage does not support. The passage is about present genetic variation. ✗
  • Choice B — English mastiffs and Yorkshire terriers differ genetically in trainability': applies the passage's logic directly — two breeds that differ in trainability must have genetic differences underlying that trait. ✓
  • Choice C — individual variation within breeds: moves from between-breed differences to within-breed differences, a scope the passage does not address. ✗
  • Choice D — comparative ranking of breeds: claims one breed is more trainable than all others, which goes beyond what the passage says. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
